intro: Sasaella bungoensis is a species of rhizomatous evergreen bamboo native to East Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Sasaella bungoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its pharmacological properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sasaella bungoensis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and creams for topical application.
